ucs.title: [LLM] ToddyCat (G1022) Exchange/IIS post-exploitation: w3wp.exe → cmd/PowerShell → scheduled task
ucs.description: Hunts ToddyCat's signature initial access chain: exploitation of internet-facing Microsoft Exchange (ProxyLogon-class) where the IIS worker process w3wp.exe (or umworkerprocess.exe) spawns interactive shells that drop loaders and create persistence via schtasks. Kaspersky tied this exact w3wp.exe-spawn-shell-then-schtasks chain to ToddyCat's deployment of the Samurai backdoor and Ninja implant on government/defense targets across Europe and Asia.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc values(Processes.process_id) as child_pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe","MSExchangeFrontendTransport.exe")) (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","schtasks.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*schtasks*/create*","*-EncodedCommand*","*DownloadString*","*Add-MpPreference*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i).*\$$") | sort -lastTime
ucs.kql:
// ToddyCat (G1022) – Exchange/IIS w3wp.exe spawning interactive shells & schtasks
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","hostname.exe","netstat.exe","quser.exe"]);
let ShellBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe","MSExchangeFrontendTransport.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (ShellBins) or FileName in~ (ReconBins)
| extend SuspiciousCmd = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc "," -ec ","FromBase64String","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-Expression","Add-MpPreference","-ExclusionPath"), "powershell-loader",
    FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("/create","/sc"), "persistence-task",
    FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "add", "reg-mod",
    FileName in~ (ReconBins), "recon",
    "shell-spawn")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          IISWorker = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          IISWorkerPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          IISWorkerCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildProcess = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildPath = FolderPath,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          SuspiciousCmd,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// ToddyCat (G1022) – Exchange/IIS w3wp.exe spawning interactive shells & schtasks
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","hostname.exe","netstat.exe","quser.exe"]);
let ShellBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| extend ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in~ ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe","MSExchangeFrontendTransport.exe")
| where ChildLeaf in~ (ShellBins) or ChildLeaf in~ (ReconBins)
| extend SuspiciousCmd = case(
    CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc "," -ec ","FromBase64String","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-Expression","Add-MpPreference","-ExclusionPath"), "powershell-loader",
    ChildLeaf =~ "schtasks.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("/create","/sc"), "persistence-task",
    ChildLeaf =~ "reg.exe" and CommandLine has "add", "reg-mod",
    ChildLeaf in~ (ReconBins), "recon",
    "shell-spawn")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          IISWorker = ParentLeaf,
          ParentProcessName,
          ChildProcess = ChildLeaf,
          NewProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          SuspiciousCmd,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: ToddyCat (G1022) Exchange/IIS w3wp.exe Spawning Interactive Shell or Scheduled Task
id: 8e7b7bcf-4c3d-4f26-9b2e-c7c4f55c8b21
status: experimental
description: Detects ToddyCat post-exploitation of internet-facing Exchange/IIS where the worker process spawns shells, schtasks or recon binaries — chain associated with deployment of the Samurai backdoor and Ninja implant.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1022/
  - https://securelist.com/toddycat/106799/
  - https://securelist.com/toddycat-keep-calm-and-check-logs/110696/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.g1022
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_iis:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\umworkerprocess.exe'
      - '\MSExchangeFrontendTransport.exe'
  child_shell_or_recon:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent_iis and child_shell_or_recon and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - ComputerName
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange admin scripts and CU/SU installers (rare; whitelist by signed parent path and known admin accounts)
level: high
ucs.rationale: ToddyCat's Kaspersky-documented entry point is exploiting unpatched Exchange servers (ProxyLogon era) and pivoting through IIS workers. Generic 'PowerShell from w3wp' templates exist, but this rule pins ToddyCat's specific chain: IIS worker → recon trio (whoami/systeminfo/net) → schtasks /create for persistence + powershell loader for next-stage Ninja/Samurai. The schtasks + reg-add + encoded-PS c

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

ucs.title: [LLM] ToddyCat (G1022) staged-archive exfiltration via xcopy/7z to cloud (Mega/OneDrive/Dropbox)
ucs.description: Hunts ToddyCat's documented end-of-intrusion data theft pattern: collected files are first staged with xcopy/copy into an obscure subdirectory (often under \PerfLogs\, \ProgramData\, recycler, or a hidden temp folder), compressed with rar.exe / 7z.exe / WinRAR using a password, then exfiltrated to public cloud storage (Mega, OneDrive, Dropbox, pCloud) — Kaspersky observed this exact sequence in ToddyCat's 'Cuthead' / Pcexter exfil tooling against APAC government/military victims.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as archive_cmds values(Processes.dest) as archive_host from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","7zr.exe","makecab.exe")) (Processes.process IN ("*-hp*","*-p*","*a -r*","* -ep1*","*-mx*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i).*\$$") | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as cloud_dest values(All_Traffic.app) as cloud_app sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_category="cloud_storage" OR All_Traffic.dest IN ("*.mega.nz","*.mega.io","*.dropbox.com","*.dropboxusercontent.com","*.onedrive.live.com","*.sharepoint.com","*.pcloud.com","*.4sync.com","*.gofile.io")) by All_Traffic.src span=10m | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | where bytes_out > 5000000 | sort -lastTime
ucs.kql:
// ToddyCat (G1022) – password-protected archive followed by cloud-storage egress
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let WindowSec = 1800;        // archive → exfil within 30 min
let CloudHosts = dynamic(["mega.nz","mega.io","mega.co.nz","dropbox.com","dropboxusercontent.com","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","sharepoint.com","pcloud.com","4sync.com","gofile.io","transfer.sh","anonfiles.com","send.exploit.in"]);
let ArchiveBins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","7zr.exe","makecab.exe"]);
let Archives =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (ArchiveBins)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-hp","-p"," a ","-ep1","-mx","-r ","-r0")
    | extend ArchiveHints = extract_all(@"(?i)\b([\w\-\.]+\.(?:rar|7z|zip|cab))\b", ProcessCommandLine)
    | project ArchiveTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ArchiveBin = FileName, ArchiveCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ArchiveParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, ArchiveHints;
let CloudEgress =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudHosts)
    | project EgressTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort,
              EgressProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              EgressCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
Archives
| join kind=inner CloudEgress on DeviceId
| where EgressTime between (ArchiveTime .. ArchiveTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project ArchiveTime, EgressTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', EgressTime, ArchiveTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          ArchiveBin, ArchiveCmd, ArchiveParent, ArchiveHints,
          EgressProc, EgressCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| order by ArchiveTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// ToddyCat (G1022) – password-protected archive followed by cloud-storage egress
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let WindowSec = 1800;
let CloudHosts = dynamic(["mega.nz","mega.io","mega.co.nz","dropbox.com","dropboxusercontent.com","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","sharepoint.com","pcloud.com","4sync.com","gofile.io","transfer.sh","anonfiles.com"]);
let ArchiveBins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","7zr.exe","makecab.exe"]);
let Archives =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildLeaf in~ (ArchiveBins)
    | where CommandLine has_any ("-hp","-p"," a ","-ep1","-mx","-r ","-r0")
    | project ArchiveTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ArchiveBin = ChildLeaf, ArchiveCmd = CommandLine,
              ArchiveParent = ParentProcessName;
let CloudEgress =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where isnotempty(RequestURL) or isnotempty(DestinationHostName)
    | where (DestinationHostName has_any (CloudHosts)) or (RequestURL has_any (CloudHosts))
    | project EgressTime = TimeGenerated, SourceIP, SourceHostName,
              RemoteUrl = coalesce(RequestURL, DestinationHostName),
              DestinationIP, SentBytes;
Archives
| join kind=inner CloudEgress on $left.Computer == $right.SourceHostName
| where EgressTime between (ArchiveTime .. ArchiveTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project ArchiveTime, EgressTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', EgressTime, ArchiveTime),
          Computer, Account,
          ArchiveBin, ArchiveCmd, ArchiveParent,
          RemoteUrl, DestinationIP, SentBytes
| order by ArchiveTime desc
ucs.rationale: ToddyCat's Kaspersky-documented exfil tradecraft uses password-protected RAR/7z archives staged in non-user paths, then carried out by purpose-built tools (Cuthead, Pcexter) to public cloud — specifically Mega and Dropbox in their 2023 APAC defense intrusions. Each behaviour alone (archive creation, cloud upload) is medium-noise; the time-window correlation of 'password-protected archive followed
